recallDetails.groundTruthDescription: 1,100+ Cancel Guides — clarity issue flagged twice with different wording

allIssues.issue_description: credibility: The homepage advertises "✅ 1,100+ cancel guides" and "View all 1,100+ cancel guides →", while multiple comparison pages state "Cancel links for 440+ services" (e.g. "Cancel links for 440+ services" on /vs-rocket-money). This makes it unclear whether Just Cancel supports ~440 services or 1,100+ services and suggests the 440+ figure is outdated or inconsistent with the broader claim of 1,100+ guides.
allIssues.suggested_fix: Clarify and align the service-count messaging by either updating comparison pages to match the current "1,100+" coverage, or explicitly explain the difference (e.g. 440+ with direct cancel links vs 1,100+ total guides) so users are not confused about how many services are actually supported.

Homepage: "✅ 1,100+ cancel guides" ... "View all 1,100+ cancel guides →" vs /vs-rocket-money: "

Cancel links for 440+ services

This discrepancy makes it unclear whether coverage is 440+ or 1,189+ services and implies some pages are using outdated numbers.
allIssues.suggested_fix: Ensure all pages consistently reference the current number of supported services (e.g. 1,189+ or a rounded "1,100+") or clearly distinguish between subsets (such as services with direct cancel links vs total guides) so that users see a single, accurate coverage figure.

allIssues.issue_description: credibility: Section "Better load speeds & SEO" says "Lottie animations are 600% lighter than GIFs"; a file cannot literally be "600% lighter" (this implies negative size), whereas other parts of the site describe size savings as "90% smaller than GIF" for dotLottie on the homepage. The 600% phrasing is mathematically inaccurate and potentially misleading.
allIssues.suggested_fix: Rephrase "600% lighter than GIFs" to a mathematically correct reduction such as "up to 80–90% smaller than GIFs" and ensure it aligns with the percentage reductions cited elsewhere.
allIssues.evidence: Platform: "Lottie animations are 600% lighter than GIFs" – a literal 600% reduction implies negative size, which is mathematically impossible wording.
